description: Product Foundation Claude Review Gate findings. This JSON is a review artifact; no implementation is provided by GL-075.
review_scope: Architecture consistency across GL-064 through GL-073
Observability / structured logging readiness for implementation
Backup / restore / data-lifecycle readiness for implementation
Operational runbook / incident-response readiness for implementation
Test strategy sufficiency for the first implementation cut

findings.summary: API/OpenAPI contract hardening review (GL-064) correctly separates Pilot-Ready from Production-Ready. Contract freeze process is design-only and appropriately deferred until after pilot feedback.
findings.recommendation: Do not implement API contract changes in the first cut. Reserve contract freeze work for a later dedicated issue.

findings.summary: Product architecture extension boundaries (GL-065) are coherent. Stable Product Core and extension surfaces are well-defined. Adapter rules are consistent across documents.
findings.recommendation: Proceed. Validate extension boundary assumptions during the first adapter implementation (GL-078 or GL-079). Avoid letting optional integrations become hidden Product Core dependencies.

findings.summary: Runtime configuration environment model (GL-066) is complete. Six runtime modes, 20 configuration categories, and production fail-closed rules are well-specified.
findings.recommendation: Implement GL-076 (Runtime Configuration Enforcement Baseline) as the first issue. Keep scope to config loader, runtime mode detection, and startup validation.

findings.summary: Production auth and operator access design (GL-067) defines eight stable roles and twelve capability boundaries. Auth provider adapter boundary is correctly abstracted.
findings.recommendation: Implement operator access hardening only after runtime config, logging, and secret boundary are in place. Do not implement OAuth/JWT/SSO in the first cut.

findings.summary: Secret management baseline design (GL-068) defines 14 secret categories, forbidden locations, and redaction rules. Secret source boundary is correctly abstracted.
findings.recommendation: Implement GL-078 (Secret Source Boundary Hardening) after runtime config and logging. Include a test verifying the demo keypair is no longer used in production-like modes. Ensure redaction is in place before real secrets are wired.

findings.summary: Persistence backend boundary and PostgreSQL readiness design (GL-070) correctly separates Product Core persistence from backend-specific implementation. SQLite is bounded to non-production. PostgreSQL is the primary production candidate.
findings.recommendation: Implement GL-079 (Persistence Backend Boundary Groundwork) after runtime config and logging. Keep first cut limited to backend selection abstraction and connection placeholders. Do not rewrite migrations.

findings.summary: Observability and structured logging baseline design (GL-071) establishes a strong foundation. Structured JSON baseline, event categories, correlation IDs, and log/audit boundary are well-defined.
findings.recommendation: Implement GL-077 (Structured Logging / Correlation ID Helper Baseline) after runtime config. Scope to logger factory, correlation helper, and redaction utility. Do not rewrite all existing log statements in one issue.

findings.summary: Backup, restore, and data lifecycle design (GL-072) defines data categories by recovery criticality, scoped restore models, retention baseline, and lifecycle stages. Secrets are correctly excluded from ordinary backups.
findings.recommendation: Schedule backup/restore/data lifecycle jobs after persistence abstraction and operator access are complete. Do not implement backup jobs in the first cut.

findings.summary: Operational runbook and incident response baseline design (GL-073) defines severity model, incident lifecycle, runbook categories, escalation boundaries, and safe manual intervention rules.
findings.recommendation: Schedule incident response automation after observability metrics are operational. Do not implement monitoring or alerting in the first cut.

findings.summary: The GL-074 implementation cut proposes a sensible dependency order. Cross-cutting risks (configuration drift, secret leakage during logging, audit integrity during persistence, rollback incompatibility, test matrix explosion, premature claims) are identified.
findings.recommendation: Follow the recommended ordering strictly. Keep each issue small. Include rollback plans. Use periodic Claude Code review for security-sensitive blocks, not every issue.

risks.title: Configuration drift
risks.description: If runtime configuration is not validated at startup, downstream blocks may run against unintended settings.
risks.mitigation: Keep GL-075 small, validate all modes, and fail closed.

risks.title: Secret leakage during logging
risks.description: If structured logging does not include secret redaction before secret or auth implementation, secrets may be logged.
risks.mitigation: Redaction helper must be the first component of GL-077.

risks.title: Audit/provenance integrity during persistence
risks.description: Persistence work must not overwrite audit or provenance records.
risks.mitigation: Persistence abstraction must treat audit/provenance as append-only.

risks.title: Rollback incompatibility
risks.description: Auth or persistence changes without a tested rollback path could lock operators out or corrupt data.
risks.mitigation: Every issue must include a rollback plan; no irreversible schema migrations in the first cut.

risks.title: Testing matrix explosion
risks.description: Adding PostgreSQL, multiple runtime modes, and auth paths multiplies the test surface.
risks.mitigation: Each block includes targeted tests; full backend suite must pass with zero failures before merge.

risks.title: Premature production claims
risks.description: Successful implementation blocks may be misinterpreted as production readiness.
risks.mitigation: Every issue document must restate the non-production disclaimer.

recommended_next_issues.title: Runtime Configuration Enforcement Baseline
recommended_next_issues.rationale: Foundational enabler; all other blocks depend on a validated configuration model.

recommended_next_issues.title: Health / Readiness Endpoint Baseline
recommended_next_issues.rationale: Minimal operational signal required by runbooks and deployment validation.

recommended_next_issues.title: Structured Logging / Correlation ID Helper Baseline
recommended_next_issues.rationale: Required before auth, persistence, or incident response can emit auditable, traceable events. Must include secret redaction.

recommended_next_issues.title: Secret Source Boundary Hardening
recommended_next_issues.rationale: Secrets must be managed before auth tokens or database credentials are hardened. Must replace demo keypair.

recommended_next_issues.title: Persistence Backend Boundary Groundwork
recommended_next_issues.rationale: Database connection and migration patterns must be stable before backup/restore or incident response can rely on them.

recommended_next_issues.title: Operator Access Hardening
recommended_next_issues.rationale: Auth can be wired once configuration, logging, and secrets are in place.

recommended_next_issues.title: Deployment / Runtime Mode Validation
recommended_next_issues.rationale: Deployment artifacts can be hardened once runtime configuration and health signals are implemented.

recommended_next_issues.title: Backup / Restore / Data Lifecycle Jobs
recommended_next_issues.rationale: Depends on persistence, secrets, logging, and operator access.

recommended_next_issues.title: Observability Metrics and Alerting
recommended_next_issues.rationale: Depends on logging, health endpoints, and configuration.

recommended_next_issues.title: Incident Response Automation
recommended_next_issues.rationale: Depends on all of the above.

requirements: GL-075 runtime config enforcement must be merged and passing
GL-077 structured logging baseline must be merged and passing
A test must verify that no real secrets exist in fixtures or demo data
GL-075 runtime config enforcement must be merged and passing
GL-077 structured logging baseline must be merged and passing
A rollback plan must be documented for any persistence abstraction change
GL-075 runtime config enforcement must be merged and passing
GL-077 structured logging baseline must be merged and passing
GL-078 secret source boundary must be merged and passing
Existing admin-token and operator-token test paths must not be broken
All P0 gates from docs/production_hardening_roadmap.md must be implemented and verified
PostgreSQL must be a first-class CI target
Backup/restore jobs must be implemented and tested
Observability metrics and alerting must be operational
Incident response automation must be in place
API contract freeze process must be completed
